stem: Which of the following is equivalent to $3(2x - 4) + 2(x + 5)$?
explanation:
Distribute each coefficient:

$3(2x - 4) = 6x - 12$
$2(x + 5) = 2x + 10$

Now combine the results:
$(6x - 12) + (2x + 10)$

Combine the $x$ terms: $6x + 2x = 8x$
Combine the constants: $-12 + 10 = -2$

The equivalent expression is $8x - 2$.

distractorRationale.A: Incomplete distribution: the student failed to distribute the 3 to the $-4$, computing $3(2x) - 4 = 6x - 4$ instead of $6x - 12$, giving $6x - 4 + 2x + 10 = 8x + 6$.
distractorRationale.B: Sign error: the student treated the second group as subtraction, computing $3(2x - 4) - 2(x + 5) = 6x - 12 - 2x - 10 = 4x - 22$, but then added coefficients incorrectly to get $8x - 22$.
distractorRationale.D: Coefficient error: the student added the outer coefficients as $3 + 2 = 5$ instead of distributing them, incorrectly writing $5x - 2$.
